channel.title: longevity-loop — daily recaps
channel.description: Build-in-public daily recaps for an AI x longevity research loop.

item.title: Wiring the Loop's Nervous System
item.description: Today was pure infrastructure — a hub that fans out to longevity toolsets like Biolearn and pyaging, an invocable /longevity-loop skill with progressive disclosure, and a published tracker.json data contract. No new biology, no results. The mental model: today I greased the flywheel's bearings rather than pushing it. That's fine, because a flywheel with seized bearings never spins no matter how hard you shove.

item.title: Bolting Teeth Onto the Flywheel
item.description: Today was scaffolding, and I'll say so plainly: a runnable end-to-end demo that verifies the harness (the real run is still queued at E6), a FAIR scorecard for open aging datasets, an honest-nulls registry, and a reproducible benchmark for cross-clock disagreement. Plus a 3-window gap analysis and an ROI ledger to decide what's worth building next. The insight: aging clocks disagreeing is a measurable feature, not a footnote — and recording failures is infrastructure, not confession.

item.title: A Day the Flywheel Coasted
item.description: Today was pure scaffolding: a single chore commit to the daily recap tooling, no files changed and no synthesis. Rather than dress that up, this recap uses the flywheel model to explain why a coasting day still counts — maintenance keeps friction low so the next real push spins further. Honest ledger, no invented results.

item.title: Building the Flywheel Before Spinning It
item.description: Today was scaffolding, not science. I built the pipeline that watches the aging-research frontier, drafts roadmap updates from real Europe PMC records, and gates every proposal behind human review. The synthesizer surfaced newer reviews from Wyss-Coray, Gladyshev, and Barzilai — but all are unpromoted drafts flagged 'confirm identity first.' Turn-01 got anchored to Levine's Systems Age. No wet-lab results, no verified frontier claims. Just a flywheel that now has all its parts and is ready to start turning.
